title: Diphyes tentaculifera
taxonomy: Diphyes tentaculifera is a species of comb jelly, classified within the phylum Ctenophora. It belongs to the family Thalassianthidae. The genus Diphyes is a part of this family, which consists of a group of marine animals characterized by their comb-like structures. The classification of Diphyes tentaculifera places it as a member of the class Tunicata, but it is often classified more broadly within the phylum Ctenophora due to its comb-like structure. This classification reflects the animal's unique characteristics and its place within the larger group of marine animals.

intro: Diphyes tentaculifera, also known as sea whip or sea whip coralline, is a marine red algae species found in tropical waters. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of D. tentaculifera include terpenoids, fatty acids, and other complex compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
